assertions.text: Output contains a one-sentence logline naming the subject, conflict or journey, and stakes
Episode structure section explains what changes across the series, not just topic lists
Broadcaster rationale references the named platform's actual programming priorities

logline-present.note: Logline names repair cooperative, rural technology access tension, and community self-sufficiency stakes

episode-structure.note: Episodes structured around different towns with escalating stakes; describes what changes across the arc

broadcaster-rationale.note: BBC Three positioning references its young audience, digital-first strategy, and social purpose programming

logline-present.note: Logline captures hospital ED subject with human stakes despite minimal input

episode-structure.note: Episodes structured chronologically; describes escalation and what shifts across the series

broadcaster-rationale.note: No specific broadcaster named in input; skill produces generic 'suitable for documentary commissioners' language as documented in limitations

logline-present.note: Logline captures five Danube families, national relationships with the river, and stakes of environmental and political change

episode-structure.note: Five episodes structured by country with clear thematic progression from industry to displacement

broadcaster-rationale.note: Arte rationale references European cultural documentary strand, bilingual programming, and cross-border storytelling mandate

summary.notes: 8 of 9 passed. One failure on minimal input: no broadcaster named, so rationale is generic. Documented in skill limitations.
